task: /review current branch

<command-message>review</command-message>
<command-name>/review</command-name>
<command-args>current branch</command-args>

How do you feel about this feedback? Do you think resolving it is worth the effort and how would you wsjf against your 7 issues and 2 nits? The new invariant gate is wired into CI and preflight, but both committed proof artifacts are stale relative to the code in this patch. As a result, check_rule_disable_invariants.py
  --verify-artifacts fails, so the patch does not pass its own added checks.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Regenerate the S603 proof artifact before enabling verification — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights.rule-disable-audit-S603.json:8-10
    check_rule_disable_invariants.py --verify-artifacts currently fails against this committed file because the recorded subprocess call-site lines are already stale (for example scripts/
    audit-suppressions.py is stored as line 1202, but the current scan reports 1203, and there are 18 added/removed entries overall). Since CI and preflight now run --verify-artifacts,
    this patch will fail its own new gate until .rule-disable-audit-S603.json is regenerated from the final tree.
  - [P1] Refresh the S311 proof artifact after the demo-data edit — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights.rule-disable-audit-S311.json:7-9
    The committed S311 artifact is also out of date: it records scripts/generate-demo-data.py at line 193, but the current codebase reports the same random.Random(seed) usage at line 195.
    Because --verify-artifacts is now part of CI/preflight, this stale entry makes the new invariant check fail immediately until .rule-disable-audit-S311.json is regenerated.

    The key detail is scripts/check_rule_disable_invariants.py verifies the committed artifacts against the current tree using (file, line, code, classification), not just counts. That
  means:

  - editing unrelated parts of the repo will not matter
  - editing a file with one of those audited sites can stale the artifact
  - even inserting a couple lines above an audited site can stale the artifact because the recorded line number changes

task:
I wouldn’t treat the two medium findings as medium-severity defects.

  1. I don’t agree with the run_repo_hook.py import point as stated. scripts/run_repo_hook.py:24 does eagerly exec_module() both helper scripts, but those imports mostly set up constants,
     regexes, and functions. The review’s “JSON loading on every git operation” claim does not match the code in scripts/audit-suppressions.py or scripts/check_rule_disable_invariants.py.
     Lazy-loading would be a reasonable cleanup, but I’d classify it as low-grade startup overhead, not a meaningful bug.
  2. I partly agree with the scope-routing point, but not its severity. In scripts/audit-suppressions.py:478, _resolve_scope() uses longest-prefix matching, so a file like extension/foo/
     bar.ts would indeed land in typescript-extension-config because that scope is defined as dir: "extension/" in scripts/audit-suppressions.py:138. That is a maintainability gap. But it
     is hypothetical, and the current suite already checks known paths plus full coverage of tracked files in tests/unit/test_audit_suppressions.py:138. I’d call this a useful hardening
     test, not a medium finding.
  3. I don’t agree with the requests dependency finding. .github/scripts/verify-badge-url.py:19 imports requests, but requests is already in pyproject.toml:25, and the badge-publish job
     installs pip install -e ".[dev]" before running that script in .github/workflows/ci.yml:1057. On the current CI path, this is covered.

  For the low items:

  - 4: agree, but it’s just a comment-level clarification.
  - 5: agree it’s a tradeoff worth revisiting, but it’s configuration policy, not a bug.
  - 6: agree; this is the strongest improvement suggestion in the list. scan_file() special-cases typescript-tests in scripts/audit-suppressions.py:422, and moving that behavior into
    ScopeConfig would scale better.
  - 7: I wouldn’t count this as a finding. It’s just test-code verbosity caused by strict typing.

  The nits are reasonab
user_feedback_text:

     regexes, and functions. The review’s “JSON loading on every git operation” claim does not match the code in scripts/audit-suppressions.py or scripts/check_rule_disable_invariants.py.
     Lazy-loading would be a reasonable cleanup, but I’d classify it as low-grade startup overhead, not a meaningful bug.
  2. I partly agree with the scope-routing point, but not its severity. In scripts/audit-suppressions.py:478, _resolve_scope() uses longest-prefix matching, so a file like extension/foo/
     bar.ts would indeed land in typescript-extension-config because that scope is defined as dir: "extension/" in scripts/audit-suppressions.py:138. That is a maintainability gap. But it

task: Super job. Fix it.

Ok, two important questions before we move on. 1. What about the rest of them in this list? Are there others we can close now before they spread?  144  module = "scripts.*"
      145  disallow_untyped_defs = false
      146  disallow_untyped_calls = false
      147 -check_untyped_defs = false
      147 +check_untyped_defs = true
      148  disallow_incomplete_defs = false
      149  disallow_any_generics = false
      150  warn_return_any = false 2. Do our local pre-commit/pre-push checks match CI as intended by our invariants?

task: commit

Ok, now talk to me about this: The new invariant gate is wired into CI and preflight, but both committed proof artifacts are stale relative to the code in this patch. As a result, check_rule_disable_invariants.py
  --verify-artifacts fails, so the patch does not pass its own added checks.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Regenerate the S603 proof artifact before enabling verification — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights.rule-disable-audit-S603.json:8-10
    check_rule_disable_invariants.py --verify-artifacts currently fails against this committed file because the recorded subprocess call-site lines are already stale (for example scripts/
    audit-suppressions.py is stored as line 1202, but the current scan reports 1203, and there are 18 added/removed entries overall). Since CI and preflight now run --verify-artifacts,
    this patch will fail its own new gate until .rule-disable-audit-S603.json is regenerated from the final tree.
  - [P1] Refresh the S311 proof artifact after the demo-data edit — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights.rule-disable-audit-S311.json:7-9
    The committed S311 artifact is also out of date: it records scripts/generate-demo-data.py at line 193, but the current codebase reports the same random.Random(seed) usage at line 195.
    Because --verify-artifacts is now part of CI/preflight, this stale entry makes the new invariant check fail immediately until .rule-disable-audit-S311.json is regenerated.

    The key detail is scripts/check_rule_disable_invariants.py verifies the committed artifacts against the current tree using (file, line, code, classification), not just counts. That
  means:

  - editing unrelated parts of the repo will not matter
  - editing a file with one of those audited sites can stale the artifact
  - even inserting a couple lines above an audited site can stale the artifact because the recorded line number changes

  So this is not an “endless loop” across all future PRs, but it is a recurring maintenance chore for any PR
